features.featureTitle: In `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`, add React Query hooks for the timesheet feature: `useTimesheets` (query — fetches all timesheets visible to the current user), `useSubmitTimesheet` (mutation — calls `submitTimesheet` backend endpoint, maps #ok/#err to resolved/rejected), `useManagerReviewTimesheet` (mutation — calls `managerReviewTimesheet`, maps #ok/#err), `useHrReviewTimesheet` (mutation — calls `hrReviewTimesheet`, maps #ok/#err). On success of any mutation, invalidate the timesheets query cache.

Create `frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeTimesheetPage.tsx`. This page allows employees to build and submit a weekly timesheet. The UI must include: a week selector (defaults to current week), a table with one row per day of the selected week (Monday–Sunday) showing date, start time input, end time input, and auto-calculated hours, a running total hours display, a 'Submit Timesheet' button that calls `useSubmitTimesheet`, inline validation errors if start/end times are missing for any filled row, a toast notification on successful submission, and a section below the form listing the employee's previously submitted timesheets with their status badges (#submitted = 'Pending Manager Review', #managerApproved = 'Pending HR Review', #managerDenied = 'Denied by Manager', #hrApproved = 'Approved', #hrDenied = 'Denied by HR'). Match the dark navy/amber visual theme.

Create `frontend/src/pages/manager/ManagerTimesheetPage.tsx`. This page shows the manager a list of all timesheets submitted by their team members, split into two sections: 'Pending Your Review' (status = #submitted) and 'Reviewed / Processed' (all other statuses). For each pending timesheet, show employee name, week date range, total hours, and Approve/Deny action buttons. Clicking Approve calls `useManagerReviewTimesheet` with approved=true and an optional comment. Clicking Deny opens a small inline modal requiring a denial comment, then calls `useManagerReviewTimesheet` with approved=false and the comment. Show a toast on success or error. Reviewed timesheets show their current status badge and any comments. Match the dark navy/amber visual theme.

Create `frontend/src/pages/hr/HRTimesheetPage.tsx`. This page shows HR Admin all timesheets that have been manager-approved (status = #managerApproved) in a 'Pending Final HR Approval' section, and all other processed timesheets (all statuses) in an 'All Timesheets' section with filtering by status and employee name search. For each manager-approved timesheet, show employee name, week date range, total hours, manager comment, and Final Approve/Final Deny action buttons. Clicking Final Approve calls `useHrReviewTimesheet` with approved=true. Clicking Final Deny opens an inline modal requiring an HR comment, then calls `useHrReviewTimesheet` with approved=false. Show a toast on success or error. Match the dark navy/amber visual theme.

Add a 'Timesheets' navigation link to the HR Admin sidebar in `frontend/src/layouts/HRAdminLayout.tsx` pointing to `/hr/timesheets`. Register the route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` under the HR Admin route tree, mapping `/hr/timesheets` to `HRTimesheetPage`. Add a 'Timesheets' navigation link to the Manager sidebar in `frontend/src/layouts/ManagerLayout.tsx` pointing to `/manager/timesheets`. Register the route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` under the Manager route tree, mapping `/manager/timesheets` to `ManagerTimesheetPage`. Add a 'Timesheet' navigation link to the Employee sidebar in `frontend/src/layouts/EmployeeLayout.tsx` pointing to `/employee/timesheet`. Register the route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` under the Employee route tree, mapping `/employee/timesheet` to `EmployeeTimesheetPage`.

Ensure that when a schedule is published (shift status transitions from #draft to #published via `publishSchedule`), the employee's schedule page (`frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeSchedulePage.tsx`) and employee dashboard (`frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeDashboard.tsx`) immediately reflect the newly published shifts without requiring a page reload. The `useShifts` or equivalent query used in those pages must refetch published shifts automatically — either via a short polling interval (e.g., every 30 seconds) or by ensuring the React Query stale time is set to 0 so the data is always considered stale and refetched on mount. Published shifts must be visible to employees the moment they navigate to their schedule page after a publish action.
